coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in should test whether Nina’s recent high output is still being sustained through unhealthy load and under-supported senior-level influence.
openingQuestion: What is still pulling you into late-night work now that the sprint push is over, and where would more visibility or backing help most?
listenFor: whether she names specific work that still spills past hours versus speaking only in generalities
whether if they describe visibility friction, they point to a concrete stakeholder, forum, or decision path
whether she volunteers clear near-term tradeoffs she wants to make or keeps treating everything as equally urgent
avoid: do not praise the feature wins in a way that normalizes the late-night pattern
do not jump straight into fixing stakeholder visibility before you know what support she actually wants from you
goodOutcome: You and Nina have agreed which near-term work a Senior Frontend Engineer should actively deprioritize or delegate, plus one visibility or influence gap where you will provide specific backing.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one concrete example of her recent late-night output and one moment where her contribution may not have been visible, so you can ask from facts instead of impressions.
